root_text.an7.51:1.3: katamo ca so, bhikkhave, saṁyogo visaṁyogo dhammapariyāyo?
root_text.an7.51:2.1: Itthī, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ itthindriyaṁ manasi karoti—
root_text.an7.51:2.2: itthikuttaṁ itthākappaṁ itthividhaṁ itthicchandaṁ itthissaraṁ itthālaṅkāraṁ.
root_text.an7.51:2.4: Sā tattha rattā tatrābhiratā bahiddhā purisindriyaṁ manasi karoti—
root_text.an7.51:2.5: purisakuttaṁ purisākappaṁ purisavidhaṁ purisacchandaṁ purisassaraṁ purisālaṅkāraṁ.
root_text.an7.51:2.7: Sā tattha rattā tatrābhiratā bahiddhā saṁyogaṁ ākaṅkhati.
root_text.an7.51:2.8: Yañcassā saṁyogapaccayā uppajjati sukhaṁ somanassaṁ tañca ākaṅkhati.
root_text.an7.51:2.9: Itthatte, bhikkhave, abhiratā sattā purisesu saṁyogaṁ gatā.
root_text.an7.51:2.10: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, itthī itthattaṁ nātivattati.
root_text.an7.51:3.1: Puriso, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ purisindriyaṁ manasi karoti—
root_text.an7.51:3.2: purisakuttaṁ purisākappaṁ purisavidhaṁ purisacchandaṁ purisassaraṁ purisālaṅkāraṁ.
root_text.an7.51:3.4: So tattha ratto tatrābhirato bahiddhā itthindriyaṁ manasi karoti—
root_text.an7.51:3.5: itthikuttaṁ itthākappaṁ itthividhaṁ itthicchandaṁ itthissaraṁ itthālaṅkāraṁ.
root_text.an7.51:3.7: So tattha ratto tatrābhirato bahiddhā saṁyogaṁ ākaṅkhati.
root_text.an7.51:3.8: Yañcassa saṁyogapaccayā uppajjati sukhaṁ somanassaṁ tañca ākaṅkhati.
root_text.an7.51:3.9: Purisatte, bhikkhave, abhiratā sattā itthīsu saṁyogaṁ gatā.
root_text.an7.51:3.10: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puriso purisattaṁ nātivattati.
root_text.an7.51:4.2: Itthī, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ itthindriyaṁ na manasi karoti—
root_text.an7.51:4.3: itthikuttaṁ itthākappaṁ itthividhaṁ itthicchandaṁ itthissaraṁ itthālaṅkāraṁ.
root_text.an7.51:4.4: Sā tattha na rajjati, sā tatra nābhiramati.
root_text.an7.51:4.5: Sā tattha arattā tatra anabhiratā bahiddhā purisindriyaṁ na manasi karoti—
root_text.an7.51:4.6: purisakuttaṁ purisākappaṁ purisavidhaṁ purisacchandaṁ purisassaraṁ purisālaṅkāraṁ.
root_text.an7.51:4.7: Sā tattha na rajjati, tatra nābhiramati.
root_text.an7.51:4.8: Sā tattha arattā tatra anabhiratā bahiddhā saṁyogaṁ nākaṅkhati.
root_text.an7.51:4.9: Yañcassā saṁyogapaccayā uppajjati sukhaṁ somanassaṁ tañca nākaṅkhati.
root_text.an7.51:4.10: Itthatte, bhikkhave, anabhiratā sattā purisesu visaṁyogaṁ gatā.
root_text.an7.51:4.11: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, itthī itthattaṁ ativattati.
root_text.an7.51:5.1: Puriso, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ purisindriyaṁ na manasi karoti—
root_text.an7.51:5.2: purisakuttaṁ purisākappaṁ purisavidhaṁ purisacchandaṁ purisassaraṁ purisālaṅkāraṁ.
root_text.an7.51:5.3: So tattha na rajjati, so tatra nābhiramati.
root_text.an7.51:5.4: So tattha aratto tatra anabhirato bahiddhā itthindriyaṁ na manasi karoti—
root_text.an7.51:5.5: itthikuttaṁ itthākappaṁ itthividhaṁ itthicchandaṁ itthissaraṁ itthālaṅkāraṁ.
root_text.an7.51:5.6: So tattha na rajjati, tatra nābhiramati.
root_text.an7.51:5.7: So tattha aratto tatra anabhirato bahiddhā saṁyogaṁ nākaṅkhati.
root_text.an7.51:5.8: Yañcassa saṁyogapaccayā uppajjati sukhaṁ somanassaṁ tañca nākaṅkhati.
root_text.an7.51:5.9: Purisatte, bhikkhave, anabhiratā sattā itthīsu visaṁyogaṁ gatā.
root_text.an7.51:5.10: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puriso purisattaṁ ativattati.
root_text.an7.51:5.12: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhave, saṁyogo visaṁyogo dhammapariyāyo”ti.

translation_text.an7.51:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you an exposition of the teaching on yoking and unyoking.
translation_text.an7.51:1.2: Listen and apply your mind well, I will speak. …
translation_text.an7.51:1.3: And what is the exposition of the teaching on yoking and unyoking?
translation_text.an7.51:2.1: A woman focuses on her own femininity:
translation_text.an7.51:2.2: her feminine moves, feminine appearance, feminine ways, feminine desires, feminine voice, and feminine adornment.
translation_text.an7.51:2.3: She’s stimulated by this and takes pleasure in it.
translation_text.an7.51:2.4: So she focuses on the masculinity of others:
translation_text.an7.51:2.5: masculine moves, masculine appearance, masculine ways, masculine desires, masculine voice, and masculine adornment.
translation_text.an7.51:2.6: She’s stimulated by this and takes pleasure in it.
translation_text.an7.51:2.7: So she desires to yoke herself to another.
translation_text.an7.51:2.8: And she desires the pleasure and happiness that comes from such yoking.
translation_text.an7.51:2.9: Sentient beings who relish their femininity are yoked to men.
translation_text.an7.51:2.10: This is how a woman does not transcend her femininity.
translation_text.an7.51:3.1: A man focuses on his own masculinity:
translation_text.an7.51:3.2: his masculine moves, masculine appearance, masculine ways, masculine desires, masculine voice, and masculine adornment.
translation_text.an7.51:3.3: He’s stimulated by this and takes pleasure in it.
translation_text.an7.51:3.4: So he focuses on the femininity of others:
translation_text.an7.51:3.5: feminine moves, feminine appearance, feminine ways, feminine desires, feminine voice, and feminine adornment.
translation_text.an7.51:3.6: He’s stimulated by this and takes pleasure in it.
translation_text.an7.51:3.7: So he desires to yoke himself to another.
translation_text.an7.51:3.8: And he desires the pleasure and happiness that comes from such yoking.
translation_text.an7.51:3.9: Sentient beings who relish their masculinity are yoked to women.
translation_text.an7.51:3.10: This is how a man does not transcend his masculinity.
translation_text.an7.51:3.11: This is how yoking comes about.
translation_text.an7.51:4.1: And how does unyoking come about?
translation_text.an7.51:4.2: A woman doesn’t focus on her own femininity:
translation_text.an7.51:4.3: her feminine moves, feminine appearance, feminine ways, feminine desires, feminine voice, and feminine adornment.
translation_text.an7.51:4.4: She isn’t stimulated by this and takes no pleasure in it.
translation_text.an7.51:4.5: So she doesn’t focus on the masculinity of others:
translation_text.an7.51:4.6: masculine moves, masculine appearance, masculine ways, masculine desires, masculine voice, and masculine adornment.
translation_text.an7.51:4.7: She isn’t stimulated by this and takes no pleasure in it.
translation_text.an7.51:4.8: So she doesn’t desire to yoke herself to another.
translation_text.an7.51:4.9: Nor does she desire the pleasure and happiness that comes from such yoking.
translation_text.an7.51:4.10: Sentient beings who do not relish their femininity are not yoked to men.
translation_text.an7.51:4.11: This is how a woman transcends her femininity.
translation_text.an7.51:5.1: A man doesn’t focus on his own masculinity:
translation_text.an7.51:5.2: masculine moves, masculine appearance, masculine ways, masculine desires, masculine voice, and masculine adornment.
translation_text.an7.51:5.3: He isn’t stimulated by this and takes no pleasure in it.
translation_text.an7.51:5.4: So he doesn’t focus on the femininity of others:
translation_text.an7.51:5.5: feminine moves, feminine appearance, feminine ways, feminine desires, feminine voice, and feminine adornment.
translation_text.an7.51:5.6: He isn’t stimulated by this and takes no pleasure in it.
translation_text.an7.51:5.7: So he doesn’t desire to yoke himself to another.
translation_text.an7.51:5.8: Nor does he desire the pleasure and happiness that comes from such yoking.
translation_text.an7.51:5.9: Sentient beings who do not relish their masculinity are not yoked to women.
translation_text.an7.51:5.10: This is how a man transcends his masculinity.
translation_text.an7.51:5.11: This is how unyoking comes about.
translation_text.an7.51:5.12: This is the exposition of the teaching on yoking and unyoking.”
